title: pimelea buxifolia
intro: Pimelea buxifolia, also known as box bush or shrub pimelea, is a small shrub native to Western Australia and is a member of the Thymelaeaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate various health issues. The medicinal properties of Pimelea buxifolia have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and relieving respiratory problems. Pimelea buxifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
